perturbation.original_text: 2. Recipient will not use any Confidential Information except to the extent necessary for the internal purpose of discussing, analyzing, and exchanging information about each party’s products, services and other offerings to determine whether the parties may enter into a mutually beneficial business relationship (“Purpose”). Recipient will not disseminate or disclose any Confidential Information to any person, firm, business or governmental agency or department, except as such disclosure is expressly permitted in this Agreement. Recipient will treat all of Discloser’s Confidential Information with the same degree of care as Recipient treats its own Confidential Information, but not less than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Recipient will not use any Confidential Information except to the extent necessary for the internal purpose of discussing, analyzing, and exchanging information about each party’s products, services and other offerings to determine whether the parties may enter into a mutually beneficial business relationship (“Purpose”). Recipient may disseminate or disclose any Confidential Information to any person, firm, business or governmental agency or department, if it is in the best interest of Recipient. Recipient will treat all of Discloser’s Confidential Information with the same degree of care as Recipient treats its own Confidential Information, but not less than reasonable care.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text grants the Recipient the power to disseminate or disclose Confidential Information to any person, firm, business, or governmental agency if it is in the Recipient's best interest. This contradicts the original text, which states that the Recipient will not disseminate or disclose any Confidential Information, except as expressly permitted in the Agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the extent of permissible disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Recipient will not use any Confidential Information except to the extent necessary for the internal purpose of discussing, analyzing, and exchanging information about each party’s products, services and other offerings to determine whether the parties may enter into a mutually beneficial business relationship (“Purpose”). Recipient may disseminate or disclose any Confidential Information to any person, firm, business or governmental agency or department, if it is in the best interest of Recipient. Recipient will treat all of Discloser’s Confidential Information with the same degree of care as Recipient treats its own Confidential Information, but not less than reasonable care. Recipient will disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information only to those of Recipient’s employees, consultants, and contractors who have a “need to know” the information to assist Recipient with respect to the Purpose and who are legally bound by terms and conditions substantially similar to those terms and conditions applicable to Recipient under this Agreement. Neither party is obligated under this Agreement to purchase from or provide to the other party any service or product. Recipient agrees not to copy or reverse engineer, or attempt to derive the composition or underlying information, structure or ideas of any Confidential Information. No licenses or rights under any patent, copyright, trademark, or trade secret are granted or are to be implied by this Agreement; Recipient does not acquire any right in or to the Confidential Information except the limited right to use it for the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Recipient to disseminate Confidential Information if it is in their best interest. The contradicted text contains this same language, and continues on to add 'Recipient will disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information only to those of Recipient’s employees, consultants, and contractors who have a “need to know” the information to assist Recipient with respect to the Purpose'. This subsequent restriction regarding 'need to know' directly contradicts the prior broad allowance for dissemination 'if it is in the best interest of Recipient,' creating a clear in-text contradiction within the document itself as specified by the 'Inconsistencies' definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: 6. This Agreement will become effective as of the Effective Date and will continue until the date one party receives written notice of termination of this Agreement from the other party; provided, however, that a Recipient’s obligations under Section 2 will survive termination of this Agreement and will continue with respect to the Discloser’s Confidential Information until the obligations no longer apply pursuant to Section 3 above.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. This Agreement will become effective as of the Effective Date and will continue indefinitely, irrespective of any termination notice from either party; provided, however, that a Recipient’s obligations under Section 2 will survive termination of this Agreement and will continue with respect to the Discloser’s Confidential Information until the obligations no longer apply pursuant to Section 3 above.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text states that the Agreement will continue indefinitely, irrespective of any termination notice. This contradicts the first part of the same sentence, and the original clause, which allows for termination upon written notice. This creates an in-text contradiction about the duration of the agreement and the possibility of its termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. This Agreement will become effective as of the Effective Date and will continue indefinitely, irrespective of any termination notice from either party; provided, however, that a Recipient’s obligations under Section 2 will survive termination of this Agreement and will continue with respect to the Discloser’s Confidential Information until the obligations no longer apply pursuant to Section 3 above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 6 to state that the agreement continues indefinitely, irrespective of termination notice. This directly contradicts the original text, which allowed termination upon written notice, and creates an in-text contradiction about the agreement's duration and termination possibilities, as both the changed text and contradicted text are identical.

perturbation.original_text: 8. The parties acknowledge that the Confidential Information is unique and valuable and a breach by Recipient of this Agreement will cause irreparable and continuing damage to Discloser for which money damages are insufficient. In the event of a breach or threatened breach of the obligations under this Agreement, Discloser will be entitled to: (1) seek specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief without the necessity of posting a bond, and (ii) indemnification from any loss or harm, including recovery of attorney’s fees, in connection with any breach or enforcement of Recipient’s obligations hereunder or the unauthorized use or release of such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. The parties acknowledge that the Confidential Information is unique and valuable and a breach by Recipient of this Agreement will cause irreparable and continuing damage to Discloser for which money damages are insufficient. In the event of a breach or threatened breach of the obligations under this Agreement, Discloser will only be entitled to: monetary damages and not to seek specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text restricts the Discloser's remedies in case of a breach, stating they are only entitled to monetary damages and cannot seek specific performance or injunctive relief. This directly contradicts the original text, which grants the Discloser the right to seek specific performance and injunctive relief, creating an in-text contradiction regarding the available remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. The parties acknowledge that the Confidential Information is unique and valuable and a breach by Recipient of this Agreement will cause irreparable and continuing damage to Discloser for which money damages are insufficient. In the event of a breach or threatened breach of the obligations under this Agreement, Discloser will only be entitled to: monetary damages and not to seek specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief. Any such relief will be in addition to and not in lieu of any appropriate relief in the way of monetary damages. Recipient must notify Discloser in writing immediately upon the occurrence of any such unauthorized release or other breach of which it is aware and will assist Discloser in remedying the unauthorized use or disclosure of Discloser’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 8 explicitly restricts the Discloser's remedies to monetary damages only, removing the option to seek specific performance and injunctive relief. The contradicted text is the same section (8) where it states that the Discloser will only be entitled to monetary damages and not seek specific performance. The initial version of section 8 states the opposite and allows the Discloser to seek both. The modified section 8 creates a direct conflict with itself because the remedies mentioned in the first and last parts of the text now contradict each other, thus introducing a strong in-text contradiction.
